definitions: To deal with all difficulties and setbacks calmly and without being upset or flustered.
To accept and cope with everything that happens, good or bad, with composure.
examples: She managed to take all in stride, even after the unexpected job loss.
It's impressive how he can take all in stride, no matter the challenge.
Despite the chaos, the team leader continued to take all in stride.
synonyms: to take it as it comes
